skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Coordinate implementation tasks through fresh subagents, staged reviews, and final quality checks inside one development session.
skill.summary: Execute independent plan tasks with implementer, spec reviewer, and code quality reviewer subagents.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the markdown context and are mostly false positives from code fences, SHA placeholders, and example paths. No malicious prompt injection, credential theft, network exfiltration, or executable payload was found. The remaining risk is low because the workflow asks subagents to edit code, run verification, and commit work in a repository.

low_findings.title: Broad Repository Modification Workflow
low_findings.description: The implementer prompt tells a subagent to implement, test, commit, and report work from a chosen directory. This is expected for a coding workflow, but users should run it only in trusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt clearly delegates code modification and commits. This is legitimate functionality, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported external command detections occur inside markdown code fences and prompt templates. No executable shell, Ruby backtick execution, or command injection vector was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every inspected location is documentation or a reusable prompt example. There is no script file or executable command body at those locations.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to crypto APIs or hashing logic. The visible SHA terms are review placeholders for commit ranges, not security-sensitive algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspection found no cryptographic implementation at the reported locations. The strongest related evidence is commit SHA placeholder text in a reviewer template.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts refer to review prompts that tell agents to inspect implementation code and compare it to requirements. No system or network enumeration instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are about requirement coverage, overbuilding, and code inspection. They do not instruct host discovery, network scanning, or sensitive file collection.

low_findings.title: Static Hidden File Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The hidden path appears in an example clarification about a user-level hook location. It is not a command and does not request secret file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is shown inside a sample conversation. There is no instruction to read, write, or exfiltrate hidden files.

content.value_statement: Complex implementation plans are hard to execute consistently across many tasks. This skill structures subagent implementation, spec review, and quality review in one session.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use same-session subagent execution for independent implementation tasks.
Provides implementer, spec reviewer, and code quality reviewer prompt templates.
Defines a two-stage review loop before marking each task complete.
Encourages full task extraction before dispatching subagents.
Guides final review and branch finishing after all tasks complete.
limitations: It does not execute tasks by itself without an AI agent and repository access.
It assumes implementation tasks are mostly independent and already planned.
It can require many subagent calls for large plans.
It depends on users providing complete task context to each subagent.

use_cases.title: Execute a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Break a prepared implementation plan into subagent tasks with review gates after each task.

use_cases.title: Keep Reviews Separate
use_cases.description: Use separate reviewer subagents to check specification compliance before code quality.

use_cases.title: Reduce Context Drift
use_cases.description: Give each implementation task to a fresh subagent with only the needed plan context.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a prepared plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this plan. Read the plan once, list all tasks, and identify which tasks are independent.

prompt_templates.title: Dispatch One Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Send a specific task to an implementer subagent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementer template for Task 1. Include the full task text, acceptance criteria, repository directory, and relevant architecture context.

prompt_templates.title: Review Task Completion
prompt_templates.scenario: Check completed work before moving forward.
prompt_templates.prompt: After the implementer reports back, run spec compliance review first. If it passes, run code quality review with the task summary and commit range.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Full Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this plan task by task with fresh implementer subagents. Require fixes and re-review before marking each task complete.

output_examples.input: Use this workflow to implement a five-task feature plan.
output: A task list extracted from the plan.
Spec and quality review results before task completion.

output_examples.input: A reviewer finds a missing requirement in Task 2.
output: The implementer receives the specific gap.
The task is fixed before moving forward.
The reviewer checks the corrected work again.

output_examples.input: All tasks in the plan are complete.
output: A final code reviewer checks the whole implementation.
Open issues are resolved before branch finishing.
The session reports what changed and what passed.

best_practices: Extract all task text and context before dispatching the first subagent.
Run spec compliance review before code quality review for every task.
Require fixes and re-review whenever a reviewer finds issues.
anti_patterns: Do not send subagents to reread the full plan for every task.
Do not run several implementation subagents on overlapping code at once.
Do not mark work complete while review issues remain open.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have a written implementation plan with tasks that can be handled mostly independently.

faq.question: Does it replace code review?
faq.answer: No. It adds review checkpoints and still expects careful inspection of the actual implementation.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides prompt templates and workflow guidance for supported AI coding tools.

faq.question: Why use fresh subagents?
faq.answer: Fresh subagents reduce context drift and keep each task focused on the current requirements.

faq.question: What plans are a poor fit?
faq.answer: Plans with highly coupled tasks or unclear requirements are poor fits until they are clarified.

faq.question: What is the main safety practice?
faq.answer: Run it only in trusted repositories and inspect review results before accepting changes.
